system_description: Conversational latent semantic space state-activation, traversal, retrieval, and inference synthesis over an Obsidian-based corpus.

latent_space.description: The total consumable semantic substrate available to the system.
latent_space.boundary: latent_space includes raw source content, derived representations, index surfaces, graph relations, and persisted synthetic nodes. These are different data types within one consumable substrate, not separate systems.

primary_corpus.description: The durable copy of the full Obsidian vault content available to the system.
content: scanned novels or source texts converted into Markdown
raw note files preserved in their original Markdown form
semantic_structure: file structure and path topology are treated as semantically meaningful
note frontmatter is treated as semantic metadata
wiki links are treated as graph edges

corpus_nodes.description: Derived semantic chunks produced from the primary_corpus.
derivation: chunks are broken down by Markdown structure
chunks retain references to note frontmatter
chunks retain references to note path topology
chunks retain references to source note identity

vector_index_surface.description: Embedding-backed representation of corpus_nodes and promoted synthetic_nodes.
purpose: support activation of candidate semantic regions

lexical_index_surface.description: Lexical search representation of corpus_nodes and promoted synthetic_nodes.
purpose: support hybrid retrieval alongside vector search

graph_layer.description: Relation surface initially derived from Obsidian-native structure.
graph_layer.boundary: The graph_layer starts as extracted Obsidian structure rather than a fully authored ontology.

synthetic_nodes.description: Persisted synthesis artifacts created from conversational perturbations and promoted back into the data substrate.
storage_forms: provenance links to source retrieval and originating conversation state
synthetic_nodes.boundary: Synthetic nodes are part of latent_space only after they are intentionally saved or promoted into the data layer.

persistent_memory.description: The durable data substrate available across sessions.
persistent_memory.boundary: persistent_memory refers to durable system-accessible data, not human psychological memory.

user_input.description: The user message or request that triggers a conversational perturbation.
user_input.role: Provides the immediate semantic pressure that initiates traversal, retrieval, and synthesis.

state_kernel.description: The initial context frame for a new conversational thread before a recurrent_state exists.
state_kernel.boundary: state_kernel bootstraps the first state_perturbation. It is not produced by the first perturbation.

active_state.description: The currently hydrated conversational state used during an active_process.
active_state.role: Provides the local operating frame for the current perturbation.

recurrent_state.description: The carried-forward conversational state after a perturbation completes.
recurrent_state.scope: Individual chat threads maintain their own recurrent_state.
recurrent_state.role: Hydrates the next perturbation when the user sends another input.
recurrent_state.boundary: recurrent_state is active_state after it becomes available to the next turn.

active_semantic_region.description: The currently activated region of latent_space during a perturbation.
active_semantic_region.boundary: active_semantic_region is temporary. It is not the full latent_space.

state_perturbation.description: The complete state transition process triggered by user_input against state_kernel or recurrent_state.

semantic_context_extraction.description: Extracts the semantic pressure of user_input for the current perturbation.

state_hydration.description: Combines semantic_context_packet with state_kernel or recurrent_state to produce active_state.

coverage_benchmark.description: A provisional benchmark for determining whether the semantic region activated by user_input and active_state has enough coverage to proceed.
current_scope: retrieval sufficiency for the current perturbation
non_scope: directly constraining the final synthesis style
forcing synthesis into a narrow deterministic score
coverage_benchmark.working_hypothesis: Coverage may eventually estimate whether activated nodes, overlapping semantic regions, contained regions, and bridging edges cover enough of the relevant convex semantic region to proceed.
coverage_benchmark.boundary: coverage_benchmark is intentionally provisional and should not block early implementation.

latent_space_traversal.description: Activates candidate semantic surfaces within latent_space based on active_state.
latent_space_traversal.boundary: Traversal identifies and organizes candidates. It does not itself synthesize the answer.

retrieval.description: Retrieves concrete content from latent_space using semantic_traversal_manifest.
retrieval.boundary: Retrieval selects and packages source material. It does not itself create the synthesis.

llm_synthesis.description: Synthesizes retrieved content against active_state to produce a conversational output or structured artifact.
llm_synthesis.boundary: The synthesis is the target output of the constrained process. It should preserve provenance and retrieval basis without being over-constrained by a direct synthesis metric.

state_update.description: Updates the conversational thread state after synthesis.

synthetic_node_promotion.description: Optionally persists selected synthesis output back into the data substrate.
synthetic_node_promotion.boundary: Not every synthesis_output becomes a synthetic_node. Promotion is an intentional write-back operation.

kernel_context_packet.description: Initial context packet for bootstrapping a new conversational thread.

semantic_context_packet.description: Structured extraction of user_input semantics and relevant recurrent_state context.

semantic_traversal_manifest.description: Manifest of activated nodes, candidate neighbors, graph expansions, and retrieval targets.

retrieval_packet.description: Concrete retrieved material selected from latent_space for the current perturbation.

synthesis_output.description: The conversational or structured output produced from active_state and retrieval_packet.

updated_recurrent_state.description: The thread-local state carried forward after the perturbation completes.

synthetic_node.description: A promoted synthesis artifact written back into latent_space.

implementation_boundary.first_build_target: A conversational state space over Obsidian notes where each chat thread maintains its own recurrent_state and each user message triggers a bounded state_perturbation.
implementation_boundary.initial_graph_strategy: Extract Obsidian wiki links, note nodes, chunk containment edges, path topology, and selected frontmatter values before attempting a richer ontology.
implementation_boundary.initial_write_back_strategy: Only promoted synthesis snippets become synthetic_nodes in latent_space.
